title: Editorial - The Kathmandu Post
summary: Editorial United Nepali Congress is in everyone’s interest The country has never needed a party with strong democratic credentials in the opposition more than it does today. Editorial Correction centres are crucibles of chaos. Still, they are redeemable. When the state deprives children of basic needs, it is manufacturing criminals Editorial On Shah government’s accommodative commitment framework
content:
Editorial United Nepali Congress is in everyone’s interest The country has never needed a party with strong democratic credentials in the opposition more than it does today. Editorial Correction centres are crucibles of chaos. Still, they are redeemable. When the state deprives children of basic needs, it is manufacturing criminals Editorial On Shah government’s accommodative commitment framework The government should try to listen to the broadest possible section of the population. Editorial Failing provinces could spell disaster for traditional parties Further weakening of provinces will bring more bad news for old parties. Editorial Addressing Nepal’s vulnerability to measles The country cannot afford a wait-and-see approach to deadly diseases such as measles. Editorial Can PM Shah redefine Nepal-India ties? The trip could be a rare moment when history stops dictating the future of Nepal-India ties. Editorial Government has 85 days to clear licence backlog. Is it achievable? Clearing the backlog of driving licences will show the state’s commitment to service delivery. Editorial Reform or collapse: The UML is running out of time to save itself The UML’s continued denial of the sanctity of people’s verdict will put it in an existential crisis. Editorial Trolled, threatened, silenced The state needs to protect citizens who dare to think differently, in physcial and digital spaces. Editorial Fuel frenzy Nepal cannot bear the brunt of external shocks without a buffer of energy and food security. Editorial Restoring Phewa The Shah government’s contribution to saving the lake will be remembered for a long time. Editorial Already late Rather than letting the fuel crisis get worse, the switch to electric options must be accelerated. Editorial No more delay The government should uphold the principle that reparations are the right of victims. Editorial Perils of haste The government must replace the theatre of immediate action with the discipline of durable policy-making. Editorial After the apology The government has great authority to improve the lives of marginalised groups. Editorial Follow due process The government should have faith in the legal apparatus to handle Gen Z protest prosecutions. Editorial Balen era begins The expectation is that Shah will be the architect of a revitalised and prosperous Nepal. Editorial No more betrayal Shah government has no excuse to keep TJ commissions in uncertainty like its predecessors. Editorial Beyond 30 minutes The goal must be to transform Lumbini from an optional stop for cultural travellers into a compelling destination. Editorial Ditch the paper The vision of reducing in-person visits to government offices through the Nagarik App is blurry due to institutional inertia.
